name: SYRIAN AMERICAN MEDICAL SOCIETY FOUNDATION
mission: SAMS works to serve the medical needs of patients in need, regardless of religious background, race, ethnicity, or political affiliation.
theoryOfChange: SAMS states it saves lives and alleviates suffering by delivering dignified, essential healthcare during crises, revitalizing health systems, and promoting medical education through a network of humanitarians; it also describes empowering local healthcare professionals through training and capacity-building to foster resilient health systems.

scoreSummary: SYRIAN AMERICAN MEDICAL SOCIETY FOUNDATION shows exceptional alignment and strong impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Hakam Sibai Award for Service (Syrian American Council (SAC))

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.sams-usa.net/zakat (Source: https://www.sams-usa.net/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 73% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 43/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, global health cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; high program ratio (≥85%) suggests efficient delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Seek external evaluation or track outcomes over at least 3 years.
components.name: Evidence & Outcomes

judge_issues.message: The risk summary mentions a high domestic burn rate, but the rationale doesn't explain why this is a risk or how it affects the overall score. This should be elaborated on to provide a more complete picture of the organization's risk profile.

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: High domestic burn: 73% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

risks.description: High domestic burn: 73% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

score_details.score_summary: SYRIAN AMERICAN MEDICAL SOCIETY FOUNDATION shows exceptional alignment and strong impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.sams-usa.net/zakat (Source: https://www.sams-usa.net/zakat)

all_citations.claim: SAMS maintains a 95.6% program expense ratio and provides medical relief regardless of background.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and maintains a specific Zakat policy.
The organization reported total revenue of $61,395,982 and a fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised.
SAMS operates medical missions and provides specialized mental health services inside Syria.
all_citations.source_name: Human Devastation Syndrome: The Impact of Conflict on Mental Health

all_citations.claim: SAMS provides support groups for children in Jordan and Syria.
Therapeutic interventions by SAMS have led to a reduction in PTSD symptoms among patients.
The organization offers multiple donation methods including Zakat-specific giving.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: SAMS earns a high overall score due to its direct service model, exceptional financial efficiency, and clear alignment with urgent global health needs in crisis zones.
areas_for_improvement: Working capital reserves of 4.6 months are below the common non-profit benchmark of 6-12 months
Heavy reliance on conflict-zone operations presents ongoing logistical and safety risks

dimension_explanations.alignment: The foundation is highly aligned with the needs of Muslim donors, as it is explicitly Zakat-eligible and follows a clear policy for distributing funds to eligible asnaf categories [2][8]. Its mission addresses a high-urgency cause by providing critical care in regions affected by protracted conflict [4][6].
dimension_explanations.impact: SAMS demonstrates high impact through direct medical services and specialized programs like 'Human Devastation Syndrome' support, which has shown measurable reductions in PTSD symptoms among beneficiaries [4][7]. The organization maintains a 95.6% program expense ratio and operates with 4.6 months of working capital to sustain its frontline health centers [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Syrian American Medical Society Foundation provides life-saving medical relief and mental health support to vulnerable populations regardless of background or affiliation.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 95.6%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach the field
High fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.02 per $1 raised
Strong focus on Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS) with documented reductions in PTSD symptoms
baseline_narrative.summary: The Syrian American Medical Society (SAMS) Foundation is a non-political medical relief organization that operates primary and specialty healthcare centers and medical missions for patients in need [1][4]. In the most recent fiscal year, the organization reported a total revenue of $61,395,982 to support its extensive humanitarian operations [3].

all_citations.quote: Medical Missions, Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS), Essential Health Programs
Reduction in PTSD symptoms after therapy

all_citations.claim: Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid
expand services to other underserved geographies
all_citations.quote: Improvement: Expand services to underserved populations
High domestic burn: 95.6% of expenses stay in the US

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: SAMS earns a high overall score due to its direct service model, exceptional financial efficiency, and clear alignment with urgent global health needs in crisis zones.

areas_for_improvement.context: While SAMS tracks some metrics, they should <cite id="16">publish beneficiary counts consistently</cite> to allow for more accurate cost-per-beneficiary assessments.
The organization could <cite id="17">expand services to other underserved geographies</cite> with limited nonprofit coverage to improve its alignment score.

bbb_assessment.summary: The organization maintains strong governance with an independent board and audited financials.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization maintains a high program ratio and strong working capital to manage these risks.
risk_factors: <cite id="22">95.6% of expenses stay in the US</cite> despite international operations
case_against.summary: The primary limitation is a <cite id="22">high domestic burn rate</cite> where a significant portion of expenses are categorized as staying in the US.

confidence.data_freshness: Financial data from 2023; Transparency seal from 2026
confidence.notes: Strong data availability across financial and governance metrics.

alignment.explanation: SAMS is a strong fit for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="20">zakat-eligible programs</cite> that serve <cite id="21">the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin)</cite> in Muslim-majority regions.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be further improved by <cite id="17">expanding services to more underserved populations</cite> outside of current core conflict zones.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust SAMS due to its <cite id="1">26-year track record</cite> and <cite id="15">100% independent board</cite>. They maintain a <cite id="12">Platinum Seal from Candid</cite> and provide <cite id="18">audited financial statements</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should <cite id="16">track outcomes over at least 3 years</cite> and seek external evaluations.

impact.explanation: Operations are highly efficient with a <cite id="19">95.6% program expense ratio</cite> and a <cite id="9">fundraising cost of only $0.02 per $1 raised</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="16">consistent publication of beneficiary counts</cite> to determine cost-effectiveness.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Measured outcomes with documented PTSD reduction

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Directly funds medical chapters and implementing partners in crisis zones

rich_narrative.headline: The Syrian American Medical Society Foundation provides life-saving medical relief and mental health support to vulnerable populations regardless of background or affiliation.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to fulfill zakat obligations by supporting high-scale medical interventions and mental health services in Muslim-majority conflict zones.
donor_motivations: Want to provide life-saving medical care to victims of war
Value high transparency and Platinum-level reporting
Seek to support mental health (MHPSS) as a core humanitarian need
Prefer organizations with extremely low fundraising overhead
giving_considerations: Large-scale operations mean funds support broad medical infrastructure
Focus is primarily on conflict-affected regions like Syria and Gaza
High domestic expense ratio requires understanding of US-based support for foreign missions
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking small-scale, local community projects or those who prioritize organizations with low domestic administrative footprints.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that outcomes are measured (such as PTSD reduction) but lack independent RCT-level evaluation.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: SAMS uses a clear theory of change focused on providing dignified medical care to alleviate suffering in crisis zones.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence of PTSD reduction proves that medical missions provide long-term psychological stability, not just temporary relief.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a 54.2% growth rate and ongoing crises in Syria and Gaza, SAMS has a high capacity to absorb and deploy new funding.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: Syria, Jordan, Lebanon, Turkey, Egypt, Iraq, Greece, Gaza, Libya, Ukraine

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an <cite id="9">exceptionally low fundraising cost of $0.01 per $1</cite> compared to peers.

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="9">$0.02 per $1 raised</cite>, which is significantly better than industry averages.
SAMS has documented a <cite id="8">reduction in PTSD symptoms</cite> through its MHPSS programs, serving over <cite id="11">2,441 children in support groups</cite>.
The foundation holds a <cite id="12">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of disclosure available.

strengths_deep_dive: SAMS Foundation demonstrates a high capacity for large-scale medical logistics in conflict zones. In a single reporting period, they delivered <cite id="13">3.2 million medical services inside Syria</cite> alone. Their financial health is supported by <cite id="14">4.6 months of working capital</cite>, providing a resilient buffer for unpredictable humanitarian environments. Furthermore, the organization is governed by an <cite id="15">independent board of 8 members</cite>, ensuring strong oversight of its <cite id="3">$99.9 million in annual revenue</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Syrian American Medical Society (SAMS) Foundation is a non-political medical relief organization that has operated since <cite id="1">its founding in 1998</cite>. The organization specializes in providing <cite id="2">primary and specialty healthcare</cite> to vulnerable populations, particularly those affected by the ongoing crisis in Syria and surrounding regions. In 2023, the foundation reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $99,929,862</cite>, representing a significant <cite id="4">three-year revenue growth rate of 54.2%</cite>. This scale allows SAMS to manage complex medical missions and <cite id="5">essential health programs</cite> across multiple countries including Syria, Jordan, and Ukraine. Their work is deeply rooted in direct service delivery, having provided <cite id="6">3.5 million medical services</cite> to those in need. Beyond physical medicine, SAMS is a leader in <cite id="7">Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS)</cite>, documenting a measurable <cite id="8">reduction in PTSD symptoms</cite> among therapy participants. The organization maintains a lean operational model, spending only <cite id="9">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. This efficiency ensures that the vast majority of donor funds are directed toward <cite id="10">foreign grants totaling $18,286,923</cite> and direct medical interventions in high-conflict zones.
